overview.partnership_rationale: Heavy investment in AI-driven drug discovery and clinical trial expansion aligns directly with UNC's strength in biomedical informatics and health data research.
overview.contact_reasoning: Retired Eli Lilly senior executive with deep institutional knowledge of Lilly's internal operations and existing academic credibility within UNC's health business ecosystem.

basic_info.name_and_location: Eli Lilly and Company, Indianapolis, IN

unc_alum.name: Markus Saba
unc_alum.current_role: Professor of the Practice of Marketing / Executive Director, Center for the Business of Health at UNC

unc_connection.donation_history: Long history of philanthropic support in healthcare. Specific UNC donation history to be verified via UNC Office of Development.

talking_points.detail: Actively integrating generative AI for biologic design via 2026 collaboration with Chai Discovery and open-source Lilly TuneLab initiative, providing models trained on proprietary research data to academic partners.
UNC's biomedical informatics infrastructure, including the NC TraCS Institute, provides scalable data management and analytics platforms capable of supporting complex R&D workflows.
UNC Health utilizes decentralized and hybrid trial models to improve patient access and trial recruitment efficiency, aligning with biopharmaceutical needs for faster study execution.
UNC maintains advanced HPC resources and core facilities for processing large-scale genomic and proteomic datasets, essential for fast design-build-test-learn cycles.
Eli Lilly maintains a formal internship and corporate hiring program focused on technical and data-driven roles, providing a direct pipeline for UNC data science and biomedical informatics graduates.

references.title: The Regional Environment in Indianapolis: Insights from High-Growth Companies
